root_text.iti110:2.1: “Carato cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.iti110:2.2: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.iti110:2.3: Carampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyoti vuccati.
root_text.iti110:3.1: Ṭhitassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.iti110:3.2: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.iti110:3.3: Ṭhitopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyoti vuccati.
root_text.iti110:4.1: Nisinnassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.iti110:4.2: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.iti110:4.3: Nisinnopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyoti vuccati.
root_text.iti110:5.1: Sayānassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jāgarassa uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.iti110:5.2: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu adhivāseti nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.iti110:5.3: Sayānopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu jāgaro evaṁbhūto anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyoti vuccati.
root_text.iti110:6.1: Carato cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.iti110:6.2: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.iti110:6.3: Carampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.
root_text.iti110:7.1: Ṭhitassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.iti110:7.2: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.iti110:7.3: Ṭhitopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.
root_text.iti110:8.1: Nisinnassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.iti110:8.2: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.iti110:8.3: Nisinnopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccati.
root_text.iti110:9.1: Sayānassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jāgarassa uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.iti110:9.2: Tañce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.iti110:9.3: Sayānopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu jāgaro evaṁbhūto ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitattoti vuccatī”ti.
root_text.iti110:12.1: Yo ca caraṁ vā tiṭṭhaṁ vā,
root_text.iti110:13.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti110:0.3: The Chapter on the Brahmin’s Offering of the Teaching
translation_text.iti110:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti110:2.1: “Mendicants, suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while walking.
translation_text.iti110:2.2: They tolerate it and don’t give it up, get rid of it, eliminate it, and obliterate it. Such a mendicant is said to be ‘not keen or prudent, always lazy, and lacking energy’ when walking.
translation_text.iti110:3.1: Suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while standing …
translation_text.iti110:5.1: or when lying down while awake.
translation_text.iti110:5.2: They tolerate it and don’t give it up, get rid of it, eliminate it, and obliterate it.
translation_text.iti110:5.3: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘not keen or prudent, always lazy, and lacking energy’ when lying down while awake.
translation_text.iti110:6.1: Suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while walking.
translation_text.iti110:6.2: They don’t tolerate them, but give them up, get rid of them, eliminate them, and obliterate them.
translation_text.iti110:6.3: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘keen and prudent, always energetic and resolute’ when walking.
translation_text.iti110:7.1: Suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while standing …
translation_text.iti110:9.1: or when lying down while awake.
translation_text.iti110:9.2: They don’t tolerate it, but give it up, get rid of it, eliminate it, and obliterate it.
translation_text.iti110:9.3: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘keen and prudent, always energetic and resolute’ when lying down while awake.”
translation_text.iti110:10.3: one who thinks a bad thought
translation_text.iti110:10.4: to do with the domestic life
translation_text.iti110:12.1: But one who, whether standing or walking,
translation_text.iti110:13.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti110:12.1: ca caraṁ vā tiṭṭhaṁ vā → yo caraṁ vātha tiṭṭhaṁ vā (bj, mr); yo caraṁ vā yadi vā tiṭṭhaṁ (sya-all); yo caraṁ vā yo tiṭṭhaṁ vā (pts-vp-pli1)
